items.criterion: A spinner badge appears on timeline clips within 5 seconds of a task being created that targets the clip's generation
The spinner disappears within 5 seconds of all active tasks for that generation completing/failing/cancelling
Works for image generation, video generation, and image editing task types
No per-clip queries — uses a single bulk query for all active tasks in the project
ESLint passes on all changed files
Spinner visually distinct from stale variant badge (different color)
Hook follows the same pattern as useStaleVariants for consistency
No performance regression — hook memoizes properly and avoids unnecessary re-renders
Spinner shows on clips during active task (manual smoke test)

unresolved_flags.concern: Task reference matching: Reusing `taskReferencesGeneration` as the only matcher will miss active video-generation tasks for source clips. The helper does not inspect `input_image_generation_ids`, `start_image_generation_id`, or `end_image_generation_id`, even though this repo stores and later reads those fields to identify which timeline generations a travel task is using. That means spinners would fail to appear for at least some travel/video tasks.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingGenerationTasks.ts:35` only checks `based_on`, `source_generation_id`, `generation_id`, `input_generation_id`, `parent_generation_id`, `pair_shot_generation_id`, nested `pair_shot_generation_ids`, and `individual_segment_params.pair_shot_generation_id`. Travel request building sends source generation ids as `image_generation_ids` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideo/requestBody.ts:112`, the resolver persists them as `orchestrator_details.input_image_generation_ids` in `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/travelBetweenImages.ts:188`, and individual segment tasks persist `start_image_generation_id` / `end_image_generation_id` in `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/individualTravelSegment.ts:334` and `:360`. The repo already reads those fields back when reconstructing segment source lineage in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/segment/useSegmentOutputStrip.ts:136`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Immediate task feedback: The plan does not cover the repo's existing optimistic-pending pattern, so it does not reliably meet the stated 'appear immediately when a task is created' behavior. With only realtime plus 5s polling, spinner appearance still depends on network/realtime latency.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingSegmentTasks.ts:6` says the existing pending-task UI supports optimistic updates for immediate feedback, and `:61` stores optimistic pending ids specifically 'before task is detected'. The proposed hook instead relies on querying active tasks plus realtime invalidation/polling, with a 5s polling interval in the plan, and does not include any optimistic handoff from task creation into the timeline hook.

rationale: Light plan - flags acknowledged and noted. Proceed to finalize for user review.
signals_assessment: Forced proceed override applied by the orchestrator.
orchestrator_guidance: Force-proceed override applied. Proceed to finalize.

signals.idea: Show in-progress spinners on timeline clips when tasks are actively running for them (image editing, video generation, etc). When a task is created that targets a generation shown on the timeline, a spinner should appear on the corresponding clip immediately. It should update/disappear when the task completes. This should work for any task type - image generation, video generation, image editing. The spinner should show on the clip overlay (like the stale variant badge position pattern). Key questions to research: (1) How does the task system work - where are active tasks tracked? Check useActiveTasks, task polling, realtime subscriptions for task status. (2) How do tasks reference generations/shots - what fields link a task to a specific generation or shot? (3) What existing realtime/polling infrastructure can we hook into? (4) How does the video editor currently know about task completion (e.g. for stale variant detection via useStaleVariants realtime subscriptions)? Key files to check: task creation flow (unified_task_creation.md), task worker lifecycle (task_worker_lifecycle.md), realtime system, useStaleVariants.ts realtime subscriptions, any useActiveTasks or task polling hooks.

unresolved_flags.concern: Task reference matching: Reusing `taskReferencesGeneration` as the only matcher will miss active video-generation tasks for source clips. The helper does not inspect `input_image_generation_ids`, `start_image_generation_id`, or `end_image_generation_id`, even though this repo stores and later reads those fields to identify which timeline generations a travel task is using. That means spinners would fail to appear for at least some travel/video tasks.

Immediate task feedback: The plan does not cover the repo's existing optimistic-pending pattern, so it does not reliably meet the stated 'appear immediately when a task is created' behavior. With only realtime plus 5s polling, spinner appearance still depends on network/realtime latency.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 2.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.
